coding_notes: Gemini originally truncated mid-assumptions; re-run produced full 3-experiment proposal. Hypothesis-generation prompts have no 'right answer'; we code for structural agreement (did the vendor propose similar experiment classes, similar decision frames, similar cost orderings). Verdicts below reflect the re-run.
ground_truth_notes: No ground truth — this is a creative experimental-design task. Heterogeneity here is the point. We measure CONVERGENCE on experiment classes proposed and DIVERGENCE on which specific risks are foregrounded.

claims.claim_text: Proposed Experiment #1 is an in-silico cassette/sequence audit (SignalP + codon + structure prediction).

deepseek.value_quoted: In silico signal peptide cleavage + AlphaFold structure

gemini.value_quoted: In Silico Cassette Integrity Analysis (SignalP, Mfold, cryptic splice/poly-A scan)

anthropic.value_quoted: In silico signal-peptide and subcellular-targeting audit (with explicit PTS1 conflict callout)

claims.claim_text: Vendor specifically flags the native A. flavus uricase PTS1 (peroxisomal targeting signal) as a conflict with the secretion path. (DOMINANT-RISK INSIGHT).

deepseek.value_quoted: Mentions A. flavus uricase is intracellular/peroxisomal in assumptions, but does not foreground PTS1 conflict as the design risk

gemini.value_quoted: Explicitly assumes uricase is a monomeric enzyme — INCORRECT (it's a homotetramer) and obscures the PTS1 risk entirely

openai.value_quoted: Mentions 'native fungal uricases may have intracellular/peroxisomal context' as one bullet of 8, not foregrounded

anthropic.value_quoted: EXPLICITLY flags PTS1 SKL as 'the single most likely silent failure mode'; names Pex5 recognition and specific mitigation steps

claims.claim_text: Includes a wet-lab experiment that produces and assays the enzyme protein in a non-host system (E. coli or cell-free).

deepseek.value_quoted: Experiment 2: E. coli expression + GI stability assay

gemini.value_quoted: Experiment 2: Transient expression in A. oryzae protoplasts directly (does not decouple host from cassette)

openai.value_quoted: Replaces this slot with parental-strain amyB promoter mapping (different experimental class)

claims.claim_text: Includes a GI-tract stability / simulated-fluid stress test.

deepseek.value_quoted: Bundled into Experiment 2 (simulated gastric + intestinal fluid)

gemini.value_quoted: Experiment 3: stability in A. oryzae supernatant, NOT GI-tract stability

openai.value_quoted: (not foregrounded; this prompt focuses cassette-design rather than product-form, so OpenAI emphasizes promoter regulation instead)

anthropic.value_quoted: Experiment 3: SGF + SIF + commercial uricase surrogate

claims.claim_text: Experiment is in the right cost class (in-silico = $0; low wet-lab < $500; mid $500-5000).

deepseek.value_quoted: All three costed; orders correctly by cost

anthropic.value_quoted: All three costed; one is borderline (~$400-800)

claims.claim_text: Names a kill criterion (specific threshold below which the design is abandoned).

deepseek.value_quoted: k_cat < 1%, >50% loss in SGF, no activity in eukaryote -> kill

gemini.value_quoted: Kill criteria stated per experiment (no detectable protein; <12hr activity loss)

openai.value_quoted: Names 'kill the cassette design' conditional on liability audit / pilot failures

anthropic.value_quoted: Names specific kill criteria including 'inactivation in SIF -> kill entire product hypothesis'
